issues.title: toSvgString byte-parity guard omits subgraphs and 6 of 10 node shapes
issues.description: runtime.ts's new toSvgString/buildSvg helpers (svgShape, svgSubgraph, svgNodeText, svgEdgeLabel — src/render/dom/runtime.ts:1092-1296) are a ~200-line inlined duplicate of src/render/svg.ts (nodeShape/renderSubgraph/nodeText/edgeLabel). The dom-runtime-parity byte-parity test (test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts:451-473) drives ONE model only — 'A[Start]-->B{Choice}\nB-->|yes| C([Done])\nB-->|no| D[(Store)]' — exercising rect/diamond/stadium/cylinder and ZERO subgraphs. The inlined branches for rounded, subroutine, circle, hexagon, parallelogram, parallelogram-alt and the whole svgSubgraph() function have no byte-parity coverage. I manually diffed runtime.ts against render/svg.ts and they currently match (no live drift), and bounds parity holds (both pad 20 and include subgraph boxes: layout/index.ts:198-208 vs runtime.ts:1266-1267). So this is a guard-coverage gap, not a live defect. But the project's own standards (code-review-standards.md 'Serialized-runtime drift'; coding-rules.md 'Keep the inlined vnmRuntime in parity ... extend it when you touch routing') and its twice-reopened history (REV-003, hybrid TEST-003) make this exact drift class the highest-risk one to leave unguarded on a brand-new duplicated serializer that D4/'parity-guarded' explicitly relies on.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Extend the toSvgString byte-parity test (test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts) with a model that includes at least one titled subgraph and each remaining node shape (rounded, subroutine, circle, hexagon, parallelogram, parallelogram-alt), asserting handle.toSvgString() === renderSvgFromModel(editedModel, theme) plus XMLValidator validity, for both light and fancy themes — so any future divergence between the inlined svgShape/svgSubgraph and src/render/svg.ts fails here.
issues.fix_summary: Added a second byte-parity loop to test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts (light + fancy) driving a titled subgraph ('Cluster One') plus rounded/subroutine/circle/hexagon/parallelogram/parallelogram-alt through handle.toSvgString() after a drag + resize; asserts byte-equality with renderSvgFromModel(editedModel) + XMLValidator validity, and that the subgraph title, dashed subgraph border, <ellipse> (circle) and <polygon> (hexagon/parallelograms) branches actually render. The extended guard exposed NO live drift — the inlined svgShape/svgSubgraph already byte-match src/render/svg.ts (2 new tests, 278->280 green).

issues.title: Save PNG evades the zero-network url( guard via bracket-notation toDataURL + setAttribute("src")
issues.description: savePng (src/render/dom/runtime.ts:1315-1335) calls (canvas as ...)["toDataURL"]("image/png") via bracket notation and img.setAttribute("src", dataURI), specifically so the serialized standalone HTML passes the export-html zero-network guards (test/export-html.test.ts:33 & :58 /url\(\s*['\"]?[^#'\")]/i and :26 /src\s*=/i). A literal canvas.toDataURL("image/png") contains the substring 'URL("i', which the regex flags as an external url(); the bracket call obfuscates it, and a code comment documents the dodge. Functionally benign: toDataURL yields an in-memory data: URI (no network) and the img src is a data: URI, so the guard's INTENT (no external fetch) holds. But obfuscating a call to slip past a privacy/security guard is a smell and a fragile, self-documented escape hatch; the real defect is the guard's coarse textual regex false-positiving on legitimate same-document APIs.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Tighten the guard regex so it matches only a CSS url( token (require it not be preceded by an identifier char, e.g. /(^|[^\w-])url\(\s*['\"]?[^#'\")]/i, which excludes 'toDataURL(') and scope the src= check to external schemes; then use the plain canvas.toDataURL(...) call. Alternatively switch to the planned toBlob + URL.createObjectURL (D3 said 'toBlob -> download'), which trips neither guard.
issues.fix_summary: Tightened the export-html zero-network guards (test/export-html.test.ts): the CSS-url check now requires a non-identifier char before url( — /(^|[^\w-])url\(\s*['\"]?[^#'\")]/i (so toDataURL()/createObjectURL() no longer false-positive), and the src= check now matches only EXTERNAL http(s)/protocol-relative URLs (/\bsrc\s*=\s*['\"]?\s*(?:https?:)?\/\//i), so a same-document data: URI is allowed. savePng then uses plain canvas.toDataURL("image/png") (no bracket cast) and normal img.src = data-URI assignment (no setAttribute dodge). Kept toDataURL per D3's in-browser canvas rasterizing, documented in-code; re-verified all export-html zero-network guards pass.

issues.title: savePng has no error handling — silent failure on rasterize
issues.description: savePng (src/render/dom/runtime.ts:1315-1335) registers img.onload but no img.onerror (a malformed/oversized SVG that fails to load silently produces no download and no feedback), returns silently when canvas.getContext('2d') is null (if (!ctx) return), and does not guard canvas.toDataURL against a SecurityError (a tainted canvas throws, surfacing as an uncaught error rather than a clear message). This violates the NFR 'no silent failures — surface errors clearly.' The rasterize path also has no unit coverage because jsdom cannot rasterize — expected, e2e is phase 4's job.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add img.onerror and a null-ctx branch that surface a concise diagnostic (console.error and/or a toolbar toast), and wrap toDataURL in try/catch reporting a clear 'PNG export failed' message. Phase 4 e2e should exercise the actual rasterize + download.
issues.fix_summary: savePng (src/render/dom/runtime.ts) now surfaces every failure via a console.error diagnostic (guarded win.console): added an img.onerror handler ('SVG could not be loaded for rasterizing'), replaced the silent null-getContext return with a reported '2D canvas context is unavailable' branch, and wrapped canvas.toDataURL in try/catch reporting 'PNG export failed while encoding the canvas'. No silent no-op remains (NFR). Actual rasterize+download stays for phase-4 e2e.

issues.title: RoutedEdge.ports shape changed — breaking for external model consumers
issues.description: src/model/index.ts RoutedEdge.ports changed from { source: number; target: number; labelShift? } to { source: {side,offset}; target: {side,offset}; labelShift? }. RoutedEdge is exported from the public model surface, so any external consumer that reads edge.ports.source/target as a number breaks. The persisted layout.json sidecar is unaffected (it carries only positions/sizes/transform, no ports). Acceptable under 0.x semver with the 0.3.0 bump, but not called out as a breaking change.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Record the RoutedEdge.ports shape change as a breaking change in the 0.3.0 changelog / release notes.
issues.fix_summary: Documented the RoutedEdge.ports shape change as a '0.3.0 breaking change (model API)' blockquote in README.md (Interactive mount() section): source/target are now {side,offset} anchors, consumers reading them as numbers must read .offset (and may read .side); noted the layout.json sidecar is unaffected (positions/sizes/transform only). No dedicated CHANGELOG.md file exists in this repo; README is the release-notes surface.

issues.title: Drive-by NUL->space delimiter in the label-pair key slightly weakens collision-safety
issues.description: computeLabelShifts (src/geometry/index.ts:205) and its runtime twin (src/render/dom/runtime.ts:537) build the label-pair group key as e.from + ' ' + e.to; HEAD used a NUL byte (e.from + '\x00' + e.to). Confirmed: HEAD's src/geometry/index.ts held exactly 2 NUL bytes on that line (rendering the git blob binary); the developer replaced them so the working file is clean UTF-8. Behavior-preserving in practice (node IDs are identifiers with no spaces), but a space is a weaker delimiter than NUL: if an ID ever contained a space, distinct unordered pairs could collide (e.g. (A,'B C') and ('A B',C) both -> 'A B C'), mis-grouping label staggering (cosmetic only). Runtime and geometry use the same delimiter, so parity is preserved.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Use a delimiter guaranteed absent from node IDs (e.g. the '|' already used for the side key, or a control char that is still valid UTF-8), or add a short comment noting IDs are space-free by construction so the space delimiter is safe.
issues.fix_summary: Switched the label-pair Map-key delimiter to the ID-safe '|' in BOTH twins (src/geometry/index.ts computeLabelShifts + src/render/dom/runtime.ts computePorts) with a matching lockstep comment. Note: this review's round-01 premise was slightly off — the runtime twin was NOT clean; it still held 2 NUL bytes on line 446 (rendered as a space by the reviewer's viewer), which made runtime.ts a binary blob. Round-02 verified: HEAD:src/render/dom/runtime.ts had 2 NUL bytes, both twins are now 0 NUL. '|' can never occur in a node id (parser identifiers), so distinct pairs never collide, output is unchanged (byte-parity preserved), and both files are clean UTF-8.

issues.title: FR7 edge-pin interaction has no pointer-event coverage; neither FR6 nor FR7 has a real-browser e2e (TEST-002)
issues.description: CORRECTED after reviewing the untracked test/interactive-subgraph-drag.test.ts: FR6 group-drag IS exercised at the unit level — that file uses an EVENT-CAPABLE fake DOM that dispatches real pointerdown/move/up and drives onPointerDown/Move/Up, asserting subgraphHit's border/title-band vs open-interior-pans distinction, the whole-cluster group-move, the box re-hug, and that non-members stay put. So FR6 group-drag is NOT uncovered. What remains uncovered: (1) FR7 edge-pin has ZERO pointer-event coverage anywhere — the onPointerDown edge-handle branch (target.closest('.vnm-edge-handle')), the onPointerMove 'anchor' branch, and anchorFromPointer are reached by no test. The FR6 group-drag unit test dispatches with target:{} (no .closest), which deliberately bypasses closest-based hit-testing and therefore cannot reach the edge-handle branch; no e2e grabs a .vnm-edge-handle. FR7's grab depends on real DOM hit-testing of a small handle (z-index:7, pointer-events auto) layered over/near a card — exactly the TEST-002 failure class (code-review-standards.md: 'test interactive UI with real pointer events or a toolbar/pan bug hides') — and it is unverified. (2) Neither FR6 nor FR7 has a REAL-browser e2e; the fake DOM cannot catch z-index / pointer-events / pointer-capture bugs. FR7 edge-pin is a headline UAT complaint ('can't move the arrows'), so shipping its grab/drag/pin with zero interaction coverage is not approvable. (The geometry of both FR6 and FR7 is separately byte-parity-guarded and correct on review.)
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (phase 4). PRIMARY (FR7 edge-pin): add a unit test whose dispatched pointerdown target resolves closest('.vnm-edge-handle') to the handle so the anchor branch runs (assert the pinned end's anchor moves via anchorFromPointer while the other end still auto-distributes), AND a real-browser e2e that selects a node, grabs a .vnm-edge-handle, drags it to another border, and asserts the edge's path start/end changed, the pin persists across reload, and Reset layout clears it. SECONDARY (real-browser confidence): a real-browser e2e for FR6 group-drag (grab the container border/title band, drag, assert members move + box re-hugs + persists, and an open-interior drag pans) to catch real-DOM hit-test/capture issues the fake-DOM unit test cannot.
issues.fix_summary: UNIT SCOPE fixed; e2e-pending-phase-4. Extended the pointer-driven fake-DOM approach in test/interactive-subgraph-drag.test.ts: gave FakeEl a real parent + closest()/classList contract, then added two FR7 edge-pin tests whose dispatched pointer target IS the runtime's ACTUAL .vnm-edge-handle element (real dataset.ei/end) — so onPointerDown's target.closest('.vnm-edge-handle') branch, onPointerMove's 'anchor' branch, and anchorFromPointer all genuinely run (previously ZERO coverage; the group-drag tests dispatch target:{} and bypass closest). Test 1: select a node (its incident SOURCE handles show, the unselected end's TARGET handle stays hidden — both positionEdgeHandles branches), drag edge 0's source from A's bottom to its RIGHT border → asserts the pin lands {side:right,offset:0}, the other end + sibling edges stay auto (no anchors entry), the live edge path re-starts on the right border, and resetLayout() clears it. Test 2: pins BOTH ends across two independent drags, keyed by edge index. The REAL-BROWSER e2e half of REV-006 (hit-testing the small z-index:7 handle over a card: pointer-events / pointer-capture) is phase ④'s job — it is NOT covered here. [REVIEW r5 — VERIFIED, unit scope] Confirmed the two FR7 pin tests dispatch pointer events whose target IS the real .vnm-edge-handle element, genuinely running onPointerDown's closest branch, onPointerMove's anchor branch, anchorFromPointer, and both positionEdgeHandles show/hide branches (pin lands {side:right,offset:0,from:A,to:B}, siblings + other end stay auto, live path restarts on the pinned border, reset clears); FR6 group-drag is likewise unit-covered. RESIDUAL HANDED TO PHASE 4 (owns closure): the TEST-002 crux — a REAL-browser e2e that hit-tests the small handle over/near a card (z-index / pointer-events / pointer-capture) — is NOT delivered by the fake-DOM unit tests and is a MANDATORY phase-4 must-exercise for BOTH FR7 edge-pin and FR6 group-drag. If phase 4 finds a hit-test/capture defect or skips the e2e, raise a TEST finding (reopen this concern in the test track).

issues.title: layout.json/LayoutData anchors keyed by edge INDEX with no import validation (fragile vs id-keyed positions)
issues.description: FR7 pins persist in LayoutData.anchors / layout.json keyed by edge index (a stringified number), unlike positions/sizes which are keyed by stable node id. importLayout (runtime.ts:1369-1377) and the CLI --layout path (cli/run.ts:352 -> applyPositions anchors, layout/index.ts:214) copy anchors verbatim with no check that the index is in range for the current model.edges or that the pinned edge's endpoints still match. Consequences: importing a layout.json whose diagram changed (an edge added/removed/reordered) silently applies the pin to a DIFFERENT edge; a stale out-of-range index is retained by exportLayout and re-persisted as a dangling entry (accumulates). It is graceful (no crash: computePorts only reads overrides[i] for i in 0..edges.length-1, and a malformed side falls through anchor() to the 'right' default), but it is a real robustness step-down from the id-keyed positions and a footgun for the portable sidecar.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. On import (runtime importLayout + CLI/applyPositions), drop any anchors entry whose index is out of range for the current edges (and ideally re-key by from|to|ordinal so pins survive edge reordering). At minimum, document in README/JSDoc that anchors in layout.json are index-based and therefore diagram-version-specific, and bounds-guard the import so stale entries are ignored rather than mis-applied or re-persisted.
issues.fix_summary: Anchor pins now carry endpoint identity and are validated on import. exportLayout writes from/to (the pinned edge's node ids) alongside each index-keyed entry (runtime LayoutData.EdgeAnchor + geometry.EdgeAnchorOverride gained optional from/to; backward-compatible — pre-REV-007 sidecars omit them). Both importers bounds-guard + identity-validate: the runtime's importLayout and a new shared resolveAnchorOverrides() in layout/applyPositions drop any out-of-range index (so no dangling entry is applied or re-persisted) and — when from/to is present — re-map the pin to the edge that still has those endpoints (survives an edge reorder) or drop it if that edge is gone, so a stale layout.json never silently pins a DIFFERENT edge. Documented the anchors field (index-keyed, diagram-version-specific, endpoint-validated on import) in README's interactive / layout.json section. Tests: +2 static (applyPositions drop-out-of-range + reorder re-map, interactive-editing.test) and +2 runtime (importLayout drop + re-map, dom-runtime-parity.test); updated the existing round-trip assertion to the new {source,from,to} export shape. [REVIEW r5 — VERIFIED] resolveAnchorOverrides (src/layout/index.ts, static/CLI) and the runtime importLayout re-map are char-equivalent: prefer the stored index when from/to still match, else re-map to the first still-unclaimed edge with that from/to, drop out-of-range/edge-gone/duplicate. Verified parallel/duplicate edges cannot silently swap (stored-index preferred when unchanged; a `claimed` set prevents two pins on one edge; ascending Object.keys + lowest-unclaimed findIndex is order-preserving). from/to are optional + documented backward-compatible (pre-REV-007 sidecars validated by bounds only). Tests: drop-out-of-range + reorder re-map (runtime + static).

issues.title: Deterministic parity/unit guard omits nested-subgraph and pin-then-resize combos
issues.description: The FR6/FR7 byte-parity guard covers the happy paths well (single-level subgraph re-hug on a member drag, light+fancy, test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts:608; a pinned end round-trip + toSvgString parity + reset-clears, :647/:675; layout() hug + static override in test/interactive-editing.test.ts:124/158) but not the two combos the delta introduces risk in: (1) a NESTED subgraph (recursive resolveMemberNodes / runtime resolve depth > 1) is not byte-parity-tested — the recursion is char-identical in both twins and cycle-guarded so drift risk is low, but nested containers hug descendant NODES not child-container boxes, so a nested parent border can coincide with its child border (a shared deterministic behavior worth a snapshot to pin); (2) a pin followed by a RESIZE that shrinks the pinned side is not tested — the re-clamp in anchor()/sidePoint is correct on review (the edge stays on the shrunk border, no detach) but unguarded.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Extend the deterministic suite: add a nested-subgraph model to the toSvgString byte-parity loop (assert re-hug + XML validity + that the parent box contains/relates to the child), and add a test that pins an edge end then resizes that node smaller, asserting the pinned anchor re-clamps onto the shrunk border (toSvgString == renderSvg of the same edited model, edge does not detach).
issues.fix_summary: Extended the deterministic parity suite with the two omitted combos. (a) NESTED subgraph (Outer nests Inner, resolve depth > 1): added a light+fancy byte-parity loop to dom-runtime-parity.test that drags a deeply-nested member and asserts handle.toSvgString() === renderSvg of the same edit (the recursive re-hug in both twins stays in lockstep) + XML validity + that the re-hugged Outer box still encloses the Inner box. (b) PIN-THEN-RESIZE: pin edge 0's source to A's right border at offset +40, then resize A's height smaller so half-height is well below the offset — asserts both twins re-clamp the pin onto the shrunk border (start y = halfH − PORT_MARGIN, strictly on-border → no visual detach) with toSvgString byte-parity to renderSvg of the same edited model. [REVIEW r5 — VERIFIED] Nested-subgraph re-hug is byte-parity-guarded (light+fancy; Outer nests Inner, depth>1; drag an Inner member; toSvgString == renderSvg + XML valid + Outer box encloses Inner). Pin-then-resize re-clamp is byte-parity-guarded (pin offset 40, shrink the side; the render re-clamps to halfH-6, strictly on-border, no detach; toSvgString == renderSvg).

issues.title: Stale e2e comments claim the select-nudge is needed to reveal edge handles (contradicts the TEST-002 fix)
issues.description: After the TEST-002 fix (selectNode/deselect now call positionEdgeHandles synchronously), two comments in e2e/interactive-editing.spec.ts still describe the pre-fix 'nudge workaround' as current, contradicting the fix and the accurate describe-header comment at :901-904 ('the nudge no longer affects handle visibility'): (a) the selectAndRevealHandles helper's use at :935 says 'shown via the nudge workaround', and (b) :964-965 says 'select A, then nudge it to force the handle overlay to actually render (see TEST-002 above — a plain select alone does not reveal it)'. The tests pass and are correct — the FR7 mechanics tests keep the nudge only to MOVE the node, and the two dedicated regression tests (:914 select-reveals-immediately, :928 deselect-hides-immediately) genuinely assert immediate visibility with no nudge dependency — but the stale comments are misleading. Given TEST-002 itself was caused by a masking no-op that hid the true select-time behavior, leaving comments that describe the pre-fix behavior as current invites a future reader to reintroduce a masking nudge/workaround.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (test-comment hygiene, non-blocking). Update the two comments (:935 and :964-965) to reflect that a plain select now reveals handles and the nudge is retained only to move the node; optionally have the deselect regression test reveal the handle via a plain select (not the nudge helper) so no 'nudge workaround' framing remains.
issues.fix_summary: Cross-track closure: tester fixed the two stale e2e comments (move-only nudge wording) in test round 04 and verified via grep sweep; recorded in test-04.md. Orchestrator reconciled this entry.
